# Build a Liveblocks 3-Column Kanban Board (Next.js)

## Background

You are extending a pre-scaffolded Next.js 14 (App Router) project at `/home/user/project` with a real-time collaborative 3-column Kanban board backed by https://liveblocks.io/ Storage. The packages `@liveblocks/client` and `@liveblocks/react` (v2) are already installed. Your job is to wire up Liveblocks and implement the UI so that multiple browser tabs connected to the same room see the same board in real time.

## Requirements

* Implement a Kanban board on the home page (`/`) with exactly three columns, in this order: `Todo`, `In Progress`, `Done`.
* Persist the board in Liveblocks Storage using a `LiveObject` `columns` root whose children are three `LiveList<LiveObject<Card>>` named `todo`, `inProgress`, `done`. Each card must contain at minimum a stable string `id` and a string `title`.
* The UI must allow the user to:
  * Type a new card title into a per-column text input and click an Add button to append a new card to that column's `LiveList`.
  * On every card (in `todo` and `inProgress` only), a `Move →` button promotes the card to the next column (`todo` → `inProgress` → `done`). Cards in `done` do NOT need a `Move →` button (and SHOULD NOT have one), since there is no next column.
* All add and move operations must be performed through Liveblocks Storage mutations via `useMutation`, so that other browser tabs/clients see the updates in real time.
* Use Liveblocks v2 React API (`@liveblocks/react` / `@liveblocks/react/suspense`). Do **NOT** invent your own websocket layer.
* Use the Liveblocks **public** API key on the client, read from `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY`.
* The Liveblocks room id used by `RoomProvider` must be the value of the environment variable `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID`. Before starting the dev server, run `export NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=$ZEALT_RUN_ID` so the public env var is populated for Next.js.

## Implementation Hints

* Wrap your app (or page) with `LiveblocksProvider` (passing `publicApiKey={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY!}`) and a `RoomProvider` whose `id` is `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID!`. Provide an `initialStorage` that contains a `columns` `LiveObject` with three empty `LiveList`s.
* Read each column with `useStorage((root) => root.columns.<name>)` and mutate them with `useMutation`. Inside `useMutation`, get a column with `storage.get("columns").get("<name>")` (a `LiveList<LiveObject<Card>>`) and call `.push`, `.delete`, etc.
* To move a card, in a single `useMutation`, find the card's index in its source `LiveList`, remove it with `.delete(index)`, then `.push` a new `LiveObject` (or the same object — `LiveObject`s can be moved between `LiveList`s using `LiveList.move` or by removing and re-creating) onto the destination `LiveList`. All mutations in a single `useMutation` callback are batched into one update.
* Liveblocks Storage hooks require client-only rendering. Wrap them in `<ClientSideSuspense fallback={...}>` (from `@liveblocks/react/suspense`) and add a `"use client"` directive at the top of any file that uses Liveblocks hooks.
* Each card `id` should be unique and stable for the lifetime of the card (e.g. `crypto.randomUUID()`). Do **NOT** use the array index as the id.
* Read `ZEALT_RUN_ID` from the environment at runtime; do not hard-code a room id.
* Before finishing the task, **kill any background `next dev` process you may have started** — the verifier starts its own dev server.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: `/home/user/project`

* Start command: `npm run dev` (must serve the app on port 3000 within \~60 seconds of starting)

* Port: 3000

* Route: `GET /` renders the Kanban board page.

* Room id: equal to `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` (which is set from `$ZEALT_RUN_ID`).

* Storage shape: the room's Storage root must have a `columns` object with `todo`, `inProgress`, and `done` arrays. Each entry is an object with at minimum `id` and `title`. Fetching `https://api.liveblocks.io/v2/rooms/{ZEALT_RUN_ID}/storage?format=json` (with `Authorization: Bearer ${LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY}`) must return JSON of the shape:

  ```json
  {
    "columns": {
      "todo":       [ { "id": "<string>", "title": "<string>" } ],
      "inProgress": [ { "id": "<string>", "title": "<string>" } ],
      "done":       [ { "id": "<string>", "title": "<string>" } ]
    }
  }
  ```

* UI selectors (used by the automated verifier). Column keys are exactly `todo`, `inProgress`, `done`:
  * Each column must be a region with `data-testid="column"` and `data-column="<todo|inProgress|done>"`.
  * Each column must contain a text input with `data-testid="new-card-input"` (used to type a card title).
  * Each column must contain an add button with `data-testid="add-card-btn"` that, when clicked, appends the current input value as a new card to that column and clears the input. Empty / whitespace-only input MUST NOT add a card.
  * Each card row must include `data-testid="card"` and an attribute `data-card-title` containing the card's title.
  * Cards in `todo` and `inProgress` must contain a control with `data-testid="move-btn"` whose click promotes the card to the next column (`todo` → `inProgress` → `done`). Cards in `done` MUST NOT contain `data-testid="move-btn"`.

* Real-time sync: opening the page in a second browser context targeting the same room must reflect all add/move operations performed in the first context within a few seconds, without a page reload.

* The dev server must NOT be left running by the agent; the verifier will start and stop its own dev server.

**Exploring Project Structure**

I'm currently examining the project's file system to understand the existing structure of the Next.js 14 application. My immediate focus is on identifying key files and directories within `/home/user/project` to inform the integration of the Liveblocks Kanban board.

**Reviewing the Kanban implementation**

I'm currently reviewing the requirements for the Kanban board. My focus is on ensuring the three columns (`Todo`, `In Progress`, `Done`) are correctly implemented on the home page and persist using Liveblocks Storage, specifically a `LiveObject` named `columns` containing `LiveList<LiveObject<Card>>` for each state.

**Refining the board functionality**

I'm now focusing on the user interactions for the Kanban board. Specifically, I'm detailing how new cards are added and how they move between columns. The UI needs to support adding cards via a per-column input and a button, and I'm ensuring the 'Move →' functionality correctly transitions cards between `Todo`, `In Progress`, and `Done` states, with appropriate restrictions for the `Done` column. All these actions will be managed via Liveblocks mutations for real-time synchronization.

**Implementing real-time updates**

I'm now concentrating on the technical implementation for real-time updates using Liveblocks. This involves configuring the `LiveblocksProvider` and `RoomProvider`, setting up initial storage with LiveObjects and LiveLists for columns, and detailing how mutations will handle card creation, deletion, and movement between columns by manipulating these LiveLists.

part.text:
**Discovering LiveObject and LiveList**

I've confirmed that `LiveObject` and `LiveList` are indeed available for import directly from `@liveblocks/client`. My next step is to verify the exact structure required for `initialStorage`.

**Refining Card Object Structure**

I've realized that the `Card` objects within my `LiveList`s need to be instances of `LiveObject<Card>`, not plain JavaScript objects. This means I must construct them as `new LiveObject({ id: card.id, title: card.title })` to ensure proper reactivity within Liveblocks.

**Structuring LiveList and LiveObject**

I've confirmed the structure: `todo`, `inProgress`, and `done` are `LiveList`s, each containing `LiveObject<Card>` instances. Cards require a stable `id` and `title`. Buttons will trigger mutations to move cards between these lists.

**Verifying v2 Storage Structure**

I'm currently verifying how Liveblocks v2 handles `LiveObject` and `LiveList` initialization within `initialStorage`. Specifically, I'm confirming that `LiveList` can indeed contain `LiveObject` instances, and checking the expected type signature for this nesting, such as `LiveList<LiveObject<Card>>`. I'm also exploring how `useStorage` will expose these nested structures.

**Understanding Immutable State**

I've realized that `ToImmutable<S>` effectively transforms our internal state structure into a standard, plain JavaScript object or array. This means `LiveObject` is a plain object, and `LiveList` is a plain array in its immutable form.

**Defining Storage Structure**

My focus has shifted to accurately typing the `Storage` root. I've been re-examining the definition to ensure `columns` is a `LiveObject` with nested `LiveList`s for `todo`, `inProgress`, and `done` cards.

**Refining State Management**

I've confirmed the `Storage` root's type definition aligns with the `initialStorage` initialization, both accurately reflecting nested `LiveObject`s and `LiveList`s. Now, I'm diving into `useMutation` in `@liveblocks/react/suspense` to precisely understand how to manipulate these nested structures, specifically by tracing the path `storage.get("columns").get("<name>")` to access and modify individual column lists.

**Implementing Card Addition**

I've successfully drafted a `useMutation` hook to add cards, confirming that `storage.get("columns")` indeed returns a `LiveObject` allowing `.get()` access to individual column `LiveList`s. While `crypto.randomUUID()` is broadly supported, I'm now considering `nanoid` for guaranteed cross-environment compatibility, checking its presence in our project dependencies.

**Validating ID Generation**

I've confirmed `crypto.randomUUID()` is standard and safe for client-side Next.js. My fallback logic also ensures robust ID generation across environments. I'm now reviewing the `useMutation` documentation for card movement, noting the process of removing from one list and adding to another.

**Moving Cards Within State**

I'm now focused on the `moveCard` mutation. I've successfully implemented the logic to find a card's index within its source `LiveList`, remove it, and then add it to the destination `LiveList`. I'm currently validating that the `cardObject` retrieved is indeed correctly formatted for insertion.

**Exploring LiveObject Functionality**

I've been examining the `LiveObject` class, specifically its `get`, `set`, `update`, and `toObject` methods. My current focus is on confirming how the `get` method handles key-value retrieval, and it appears straightforward for accessing properties like "id" and "title" from `cardObject`.

**Refining Type Safety**

I've confirmed that `cardObject.get("id")` correctly returns a string, making type-safe lookups within `LiveObject` instances straightforward. My next step is to verify the correct import paths for `LiveblocksProvider`, `RoomProvider`, `ClientSideSuspense`, `useStorage`, and `useMutation` from the `@liveblocks/react` and `@liveblocks/react/suspense` packages.

**Implementing Client-Side Providers**

I've realized that Liveblocks storage hooks necessitate client-side rendering and must be enclosed within `<ClientSideSuspense>`, alongside a `"use client"` directive for files utilizing these hooks. My current strategy is to designate `app/page.tsx` as a client component to seamlessly integrate `LiveblocksProvider`, `RoomProvider`, `ClientSideSuspense`, and the main Board UI.

**Verifying Environment Variables**

I'm confirming that my client-side components correctly access `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID`. It's crucial to ensure `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` is exported before starting the dev server to prevent runtime errors in `RoomProvider`.

**Handling Environment Variables**

I've confirmed that `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` must be exported before the dev server starts. To prevent runtime crashes, I'll implement a fallback mechanism for `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY`, ensuring graceful handling if these environment variables are undefined or empty during build or runtime.

**Defining Kanban Structure**

I've confirmed the room ID relies on `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID`. My focus has now shifted to architecting the Kanban board, defining the `todo`, `inProgress`, and `done` columns. I'm meticulously mapping out the required UI selectors for the automated verifier, including input fields, add buttons, and card structures.

**Designing Kanban Layout**

I'm now focusing on the visual structure of the Kanban board, specifically the layout and ordering of the `todo`, `inProgress`, and `done` columns. I'm also verifying that the column labels align with their respective keys. I will proceed with CSS styling, assuming Tailwind CSS is not available, by checking existing CSS files.

**Styling Kanban Components**

I've confirmed no global CSS exists, so I'm proceeding with inline styles for a clean, professional look, ensuring all necessary `data-testid` attributes are present. I've also designed the state management for individual column text inputs using an object where each key corresponds to a column.

**Validating Card Movement Logic**

I've confirmed that trimming input before adding a card correctly prevents empty or whitespace-only entries. My current focus is ensuring the "Move →" button's behavior aligns with requirements: promoting cards only from `todo` to `inProgress` and `inProgress` to `done`, and omitting it entirely for cards in the `done` column.

**Implementing Card Movement**

I've finalized the logic for moving cards between columns, ensuring the "Move →" button correctly promotes cards from `todo` to `inProgress` and `inProgress` to `done`. I'm also confirming that cards in the `done` column will not display this button. Next, I'm defining the structure for columns as regions.

**Structuring Column Components**

I'm defining the structure for each column as a `div` or `section` with `data-testid="column"` and `data-column` attributes, and ensuring each card row includes `data-testid="card"` and `data-card-title`. I've also confirmed that `useStorage` hooks will correctly access column data thanks to `ClientSideSuspense`.

**Defining Initial Storage**

I've confirmed the structure for `initialStorage` within `RoomProvider`, including the `LiveObject` for `columns` containing `LiveList`s for `todo`, `inProgress`, and `done` cards. My current focus is ensuring this setup is compatible with the Next.js App Router and deciding on the optimal placement for the providers.

**Configuring Initial Storage**

I've finalized the `initialStorage` structure, ensuring it correctly defines `columns` as a `LiveObject` containing empty `LiveList`s for `todo`, `inProgress`, and `done`. My focus is now on seamlessly integrating this with `LiveblocksProvider` and `RoomProvider` within the Next.js App Router, determining the most efficient placement for these components.

**Refining Card Movement Logic**

I'm currently refining the `moveCard` mutation to ensure precise handling of card transfers between columns. My focus is on streamlining the deletion from the source and insertion into the destination lists, ensuring efficient and accurate state updates for the Kanban board.

**Optimizing Card State Management**

I'm refining how input values for new cards are managed across different columns. My current focus is on ensuring the `setInputValues` function correctly updates the state for each individual column's input field, preparing for efficient card creation.

**Refining Card Rendering**

I'm currently ensuring each card displays its title and includes the necessary `data-testid` attributes for testing. My focus is on confirming the correct rendering of card content within its designated column.

**Refining Card Movement Logic**

I'm currently refining the `moveCard` mutation to ensure precise handling of card transfers between columns. My focus is on streamlining the deletion from the source and insertion into the destination lists, ensuring efficient and accurate state updates for the Kanban board.

**Updating Card Movement Logic**

I'm refining the `moveCard` mutation to ensure precise handling of card transfers between columns. My focus is on streamlining the deletion from the source and insertion into the destination lists, ensuring efficient and accurate state updates for the Kanban board.

**Refining Card Addition Logic**

I'm refining the `addCard` mutation to ensure new cards are correctly added to their respective columns, focusing on input trimming and clear state updates. My current focus is on the "add-card-btn" logic, ensuring proper functionality and styling for adding new cards to the Kanban board.

**Adding Card Functionality**

I'm refining the `addCard` mutation to ensure new cards are correctly added to their respective columns, focusing on input trimming and clear state updates. My current focus is on the "add-card-btn" logic, ensuring proper functionality and styling for adding new cards to the Kanban board.

**Generating Unique IDs**

I've confirmed that `crypto.randomUUID()` is generally available in modern browsers and for local development. To ensure maximum compatibility, I've implemented a robust helper function that falls back to a combination of `Math.random()` and `Date.now()` if `crypto.randomUUID()` is not accessible, ensuring unique IDs for new cards.

part.text:
**Clarifying Storage Types**

I'm currently grappling with the generic type inference for `useStorage`. It appears the `root` parameter is defaulting to `any` or a generic JSON type because the storage schema or the `useStorage` generic itself hasn't been explicitly defined. I need to investigate how to correctly specify these types to avoid ambiguity.

**Refining Storage Typing**

I'm exploring how to precisely type the `root` parameter within `useStorage`. While casting `root` to `any` provides functional clarity, I'm investigating more robust solutions for perfect type inference, potentially by defining custom hooks or precisely mapping the immutable storage structure to avoid ambiguity.

**Defining Storage Access**

I'm currently investigating how to correctly access nested properties within Liveblocks storage. The type error indicates that `root` doesn't inherently know about properties like `columns` or `todo`. Explicitly casting `root` to `any` circumvents this, but I'm looking for a more type-safe approach.

part.text:
**Confirming Build Success**

The build has successfully compiled without any errors. I am now proceeding to verify that all specified requirements, starting with the implementation of a three-column Kanban board on the home page, are met.

**Validating Core Features**

I've confirmed the `columnsData` correctly reflects the `Todo`, `In Progress`, `Done` order and that `initialStorage` properly sets up `LiveObject` and `LiveList` structures for cards, each with `id` and `title`. The UI elements for adding new cards and moving them between columns are also in place as per the requirements.

**Verifying Edge Cases**

I've confirmed that empty input prevents card creation and that only cards in 'Todo' and 'In Progress' columns feature the move button. All mutations correctly use `useMutation` for real-time synchronization, and environment variables for configuration are properly sourced.

**Implementing Card Movement Logic**

I'm now focusing on the precise implementation of card movement. This involves a single `useMutation` call to accurately locate a card and then manage its index across different columns.

**Refining Card Movement**

I'm confirming the `moveCard` mutation's logic, ensuring it correctly deletes cards from their source `LiveList` by index and pushes them to the destination `LiveList`. All mutations are batched within a single `useMutation` callback for efficient updates.

**Confirming Final Details**

I'm verifying the last few points, ensuring the `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` is correctly sourced and no `next dev` process is left running. All UI selectors for columns, inputs, buttons, and card titles are also confirmed to match the acceptance criteria.

input.result:
I have successfully implemented the real-time collaborative 3-column Kanban board powered by Liveblocks.

Here is a summary of the implementation:

1. **Page Structure & Client-Side Suspense**:
   - Converted `/home/user/project/app/page.tsx` to a `"use client"` component.
   - Wrapped the Kanban board (`Board`) in `LiveblocksProvider` and `RoomProvider` using `ClientSideSuspense` from `@liveblocks/react` for client-side rendering.
   - Initialized the Liveblocks storage with the `columns` `LiveObject` containing three empty `LiveList`s: `todo`, `inProgress`, and `done`.

2. **UI & Selectors**:
   - Rendered exactly three columns in the requested order: `Todo`, `In Progress`, and `Done`.
   - Added `data-testid="column"` and `data-column` attributes to each column.
   - Included a text input with `data-testid="new-card-input"` and an add button with `data-testid="add-card-btn"` in each column.
   - Prevented empty or whitespace-only card additions.
   - Added `data-testid="card"` and `data-card-title` containing the card's title on every card row.
   - Added a `Move →` button with `data-testid="move-btn"` to cards in the `todo` and `inProgress` columns, promoting them to the next column. Cards in the `done` column do not render this button.

3. **Liveblocks Storage Mutations**:
   - Read column states with `useStorage`.
   - Performed all state mutations (adding and moving cards) using type-safe `useMutation` hooks.
   - Used stable unique card IDs generated with a robust fallback supporting `crypto.randomUUID()` and custom entropy.

4. **Verification & Process Cleanup**:
   - Verified that the Next.js application builds successfully with zero TypeScript compilation errors.
   - Verified that the development server starts correctly on port 3000.
   - Terminated the development server background process so that the verifier can run its own server.
